title: The Cost of Misrepresenting Yourself to AI
description: Why human–AI misalignment often begins before the model responds — in how we represent our own goals in language.

title: Expression Distortion Cost: When Your Words Don't Match Your Mind
description: Introducing EDC: the performance loss when expressed input diverges from underlying intentions and constraints.

title: Self-Alignment Load: The Hidden Work Before You Prompt
description: Introducing SAL: the cognitive effort required to identify, structure, and articulate internal states for AI.

title: The Authenticity–Cost Tradeoff in Human–AI Interaction
description: How distortion cost and alignment effort jointly shape interaction quality in the Authenticity–Cost Model.

title: When AI Misalignment Is Co-Produced, Not Model-Driven
description: Reframing interaction failure as jointly produced by model capability and user-side representation.

title: Distortion Before Transmission: The Cognition–Language Gap
description: Why structured distortion at the cognition–language interface matters more than channel noise.

title: Representational Fidelity and the Distribution of Advantage
description: Who gains from AI-mediated work when representational accuracy is unevenly distributed.

title: Why AI Outputs Feel Generic: A User-Side Explanation
description: A user-side account of why AI responses feel misaligned even when technically fluent.

title: AI Generates Options. Humans Complete Value.
description: The central claim of Judgment-as-Product: value completes at accountable human authorization.

title: Why AI Outputs Are Materials, Not Products
description: Under generative abundance, fluent outputs remain institutionally inert until judged and adopted.

title: What Is Judgment-as-Product?
description: Defining JAP: judgment as the site where value, risk, and responsibility are assigned.

title: The Generative Abundance Paradox
description: More plausible outputs can expand evaluative work faster than they reduce decision time.

title: When Production Stops Being Scarce
description: How generative AI collapses production scarcity and shifts the binding constraint to judgment.

title: What Judgment Includes—and What It Does Not
description: Selection, validation, adoption, accountability — and the limits of the JAP framework.

title: When Does Judgment-as-Product Matter?
description: Boundary conditions where judgment becomes the operative bottleneck in AI workflows.

title: Judgment-as-Product vs. Information Overload
description: How JAP differs from attention economy and information overload accounts.

title: Judgment Burden: The Work Nobody Measures
description: The hidden cognitive labor of evaluating, verifying, and standing behind AI outputs.

title: Decision Latency: Why More Options Mean Slower Decisions
description: When generation compresses production time but expands the space of responsible choice.

title: Responsibility Concentration in AI-Mediated Workflows
description: How judgment burden and accountability concentrate among human decision-makers.

title: What Is the AI Latency Tax?
description: Latency as a structural, distributive externality in intelligent systems — beyond user frustration.

title: Unequal Burdens: Who Pays the AI Latency Tax?
description: Asymmetry in how delay is distributed across users, tiers, and contexts.

title: AI Latency vs. the Attention Economy
description: Why suspended attention during waiting is not the same as attention captured by content.

title: AI Latency and the Digital Divide
description: Extending distributive analysis from access to time as a resource in AI systems.

title: Temporal Justice in the Age of Intelligent Systems
description: Fairness in how waiting time is allocated when AI mediates essential services.

title: Why Education and Healthcare Should Not Commodify AI Responsiveness
description: Ethical limits on tiered latency when AI mediates socially vital domains.

title: From Knowledge Scarcity to Learning Model Scarcity
description: When information is abundant, the constraint shifts to how learning is orchestrated.

title: Why Learning Models Matter More Than Information Alone
description: Learning model choice — not information access — becomes the binding educational variable.

title: AI as Expansion of the Learning Model Space
description: How AI widens the repertoire of ways a learner can acquire a skill.

title: What Is Learning Model Orchestration?
description: Defining LMO: intentional selection, sequencing, and combination of learning models.

title: From Personalized Learning to Orchestrated Learning
description: Why personalization alone cannot address learning model scarcity.

title: Why Personalization Is Not Enough
description: The limits of adaptive instruction without orchestration across learning models.

title: Orchestration Inequality: Who Gets Better Learning Paths?
description: When unequal access to learning orchestration becomes a new form of educational stratification.

title: AI as an Orchestration Layer in Education
description: AI's role in expanding and coordinating learning models — not just delivering content.

title: Sequence, Fit, and Combination in Learning Design
description: Practical logic for matching learning models to learners, tasks, and constraints.

title: Implications Across the Life Course
description: How learning model orchestration reframes education from schooling to lifelong learning.

title: AI Doesn't Kill Creativity. It Reweights It.
description: The motivational reweighting thesis: AI redistributes creative motives rather than replacing them.

title: Three Creative Motives That Have Always Coexisted
description: Expression-driven, feedback-driven, and outcome-driven creation in a triadic framework.

title: Expression-Driven Creation in the Generative Age
description: How lowered technical friction expands expressive action and self-directed making.

title: Feedback-Driven Creation and Platform Metrics
description: When generative AI accelerates responsiveness to audience signals and quantified feedback.

title: Outcome-Driven Creation, Efficiency, and Precarity
description: Outcome motives under generative abundance: scale, oversupply, and intensified precarity.

title: The Motivational Reweighting Thesis
description: Generative AI reorganizes activation costs and institutional rewards of long-standing creative motives.

title: Expression-Weight Restoration: Why Low-Stakes Creation Matters Again
description: When reduced friction renews the practical significance of personal, low-stakes making.

title: Feedback Acceleration Under Generative AI
description: How platformized environments intensify feedback-driven creative labor with generative tools.

title: Outcome Intensification in Platformized Creation
description: Why outcome-driven motives may deepen rather than recede in AI-mediated cultural production.

title: Why Creative Motives Are Coexistent, Not Substitutive
description: Against narratives that treat creative history as one motive replacing another.

title: Why Waiting for AI Is Not Free Time
description: Waiting for AI responses is not empty interval — it carries cognitive and affective cost.

title: The Hidden Cost of Idle Time in AI-Mediated Interaction
description: The AI Waiting Tax: psychological and practical burdens of idle time in the age of AI.

title: Suspended Attention: Waiting as a Cognitive Burden
description: Suspended attention during AI delay — distinct from attention consumed by content.

title: From Waiting Tax to Latency Tax
description: How subjective waiting experience extends into structural latency as a socio-technical externality.

title: The Invisibility of AI Delay
description: Why latency is hard to see, measure, and contest — and why that matters for everyday users.

title: How AI Delay Accumulates Across Interactions
description: Micro-delays that compound across sessions, tasks, and workflows in daily AI use.

title: Temporal Fragmentation: How AI Delay Breaks Attention
description: How inference gaps fragment attention and disrupt the rhythm of everyday knowledge work.
